content.full_text:
TERRELL G. WHITTIER
1377 YUKON TERRACE
SUNNYVALE, CA. 94087-4413

29 April 1992


Fair Political Practices Commission
428 J Street, Suite 800
P.O. Box 807
Sacramento, CA  95804

Attn:  Legal Division

Sirs:

I wish a written legal determination on a question of conflict of
interest.  I have asked our city attorney and was advised it
would be in my best interests to have you make the determination.

I worked for Lockheed Missiles & Space Company, Inc. in
Sunnyvale, Calif. for almost 25 years and retired from this
company at this location on March 15, 1991.

I receive a monthly retirement check from the LOCKHEED
CORPORATION, Retirement Plan Office, 4500 Park Granada Blvd.,
Calabasas, CA 91399-0221.

As far as I know, there is nothing I can do or say as a Planning
Commissioner for the City of Sunnyvale that can or will have an
effect on my retirement payments.

Question:  Is there a conflict of interest in sitting on the
Planning Commission for the City of Sunnyvale when the Lockheed
Missiles & Space Co., Inc. brings a matter before the commission?

During the time I was working for the company I regularly stepped
down on matters pertaining to LMSC because they were my employer.
I do not know if I should step down now that I no longer work for
this company.

Sincerely,

[signature]

408 738-4019
